---
title: "GLOA101"
author: "Byunghwan Son <br> George Mason University"
date: " "
#########################################
linkcolor: red
citecolor: blue
urlcolor: blueß
output: 
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    seal: false
    nature:
      beforeInit: "https://platform.twitter.com/widgets.js"
      ratio: '16:9'
      highlightStyle: github
      self-contained: false
  chakra: css/libs/remark-latest.min.js
css: ["default", 
      "css/footer.css", 
      "css/color.css", 
      # "css/xaringan-themer.css",
      default-fonts]
lib_dir: css/libs
beforeInit: "css/macros.js"
# seal: false  
#bibliography: /Users/bson3/Dropbox/latex/mybib2.bib
#remotes::install_github('yihui/xaringan', upgrade = TRUE)
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# to use FontAwesome
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(fs)
library(fontawesome)
# library(magick)  
library(ggplot2)
library(WDI)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, error=F, echo=F)
options(htmltools.preserve.raw = FALSE)
# library(xaringancolor)
# setup_colors(
#   red = "red",
#   green = "green",
#   blue = "blue",
#   yellow = "yellow"
# )

```

class: inverse, center, middle
background-image: url(https://www.cognyte.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Transnational_Crime_1200x630.png)
background-size: 115%
# .Huge[Global Crimes & Terrorism]

### GLOA 101

---

.full-width-blue[
# Transnational Organized Crimes (TOCs) .tiny[UNODC]]
<br>
--

### 💊 Drug trafficking
###	🌳 Illegal logging & narco-deforestation
###	💻 Cybercrime
###	👥 Human trafficking
###	🔫 Firearms trafficking
###	⛏️ Critical minerals trafficking
### + ☠️ Maritime Piracy
---

.full-width-blue[
# Transnational Organized Crimes (TOCs) .tiny[UNODC]]
<br>
--

### 💊 Drug trafficking
###	.hl1[🌳 Illegal logging & deforestation]
###	💻 Cybercrime
###	.hl1[👥 Human trafficking]
###	.hl1[🔫 Firearms trafficking]
###	⛏️ Critical minerals trafficking
### .hl1[+ ☠️ Maritime Piracy]

---

# Illegal Logging

<iframe width="1000" height="500" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/ih5ljS-3QBs?si=vEphF2vKN9J18K0a" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>

---

class: middle, center, inverse
background-image: url(https://www.adb.org/sites/default/files/styles/content_media/public/content-media/7763-human-trafficking.jpg?itok=MSvBYONb)
background-size: contain

# .Huge[**.yellow[Human Trafficking]**]


---

.full-width-blue[
# Understanding Human Trafficking
]
<br>

--

- .LARGE[🔑 .hl2[Definition]]
	- .Large[Exploitation of people for labor or commercial sex]
	- .Large[Conducted through:	Force; Fraud;	Coercion]

--

- .LARGE[⚖️ .hl2[Two Main Types]]
	-	.Large[Sex trafficking]
	-	.Large[Labor trafficking: .house servitude; construction; hacking]

--

-  .LARGE[🧩 .hl2[Core Framework (3 Elements)]]
	1.	.Large[Acts: recruit, transport, harbor, obtain]
	2.	.Large[Means: force, fraud, coercion (e.g., thru debt)]
	3.	.Large[Purpose: exploitation]
	
---

class: inverse
background-image: url(https://www.imf.org/external/pubs/ft/fandd/2018/09/images/092018/caballero-chart1-lg.jpg)
background-position: right
background-size: contain

# **Origins**

--

## - Asia-Pacific the largest 'supplier'

--

## - European 'periphery'

--

## - fewer from the rest (cost)

--

## - language barrier; <br>kinship network

---

class: top, right
background-image: url(https://op.europa.eu/webpub/eca/special-reports/human-trafficking-09-2017/img/GR02_EN.png)
background-size: 95%

- .blue[source: UNODC 2016]

---

background-image: url(https://www.everycrsreport.com/files/20191030_R44953_images_9ea6320c71c6dc53b5bdeb1cbff8b937a71b3284.png)
background-size: 80%

# How much do states care? <br>.tiny[**.pink[(State Department Report 2019)]**]

---

.full-width-blue[
# What is maritime piracy? .tiny[.pink[UN] .pink[C]onvention on .pink[L]aw .pink[o]f the .pink[S]ea, Article 101 (.pink[UNCLOS] 1982)]
]
<br><br>
--

.blockquote[
.black[
.Large[
<br>
a. any .hl1[illegal] acts of violence or detention, or any act of depredation, committed for .hl1[private ends] by the crew or the passengers of a .hl1[private ship or a private aircraft], and directed:

  - on the high seas, against another ship or aircraft, or against persons or property on board such ship or aircraft;

  - against a ship, aircraft, persons or property in a place .red[outside the jurisdiction of any State];

b. any act of .red[voluntary participation] in the operation of a ship or of an aircraft with knowledge of facts making it a pirate ship or aircraft;

]
]
]

---
background-image: url(https://raw.githubusercontent.com/textvulture/textvulture.github.io/refs/heads/master/images/piracyTrend.png)
background-position: right
background-size: contain

# **The trend**

## - regional effort: multilateral<br> responses (MSSP; ReCAAP)

--

## - made a good .orange[pregress],<br> but not quite enough

--

## - significant toll on regional .red[trade] volumes

---

# The cause of piracy .tiny[(Jablonski & Oliver 2013)]

--
<br>
.center[
![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/textvulture/textvulture.github.io/refs/heads/master/images/Jablonski.png)
]

---
class: inverse, center, middle
background-image: url(https://newsimg.bbc.co.uk/media/images/46509000/jpg/_46509314_henry_terminus_466.jpg)
background-size: 105%

# .Huge[Terroris Attacks]

---

.full-width-blue[
# The idea]

--

## Highly contested: .tiny["unlawful use of force or violence against persons or property to intimidate or coerce a government, civilian population, or any segment thereof, in furtherance of political, religious, or social objectives. It involves premeditated, calculated acts designed to induce fear, often targeting non-combatants" (FBI)]

--

### - .purple[targets: civilians vs. non-combatants]: ‘pure’ civilians?; oﬀ-duty military?; active military?; governmental oﬃcials?

### - .orange[purpose]:punishment? fear?

--

### - our working definition of global terrorism
.Large["a transnational act of violence for political purposes by organization(s) in non-combat situations." (still incomplete)]

---

# The types .tiny[(Global Terrorism Data)]

--

```{r, fig.width=14}
gtdBase <- readRDS("/Users/bson3/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-GeorgeMasonUniversity-O365Production/data_repo/GTD/gtdBase.rds")

## The types of terrorist attacks
gtdBase %>% 
  select(iyear, region_txt, crit1, crit2, crit3, targtype1_txt) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~na_if(., -99))) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(crit1, crit2, crit3), names_to = "criterion", values_to = "met") %>%
  filter(!is.na(met)) %>%
  mutate(criterion = recode(criterion,
                            crit1 = "Political, economic,\nor religious",
                            crit2 = "Coercion or\nintimidation",
                            crit3 = "Violates Geneva\nconvention"),
         met = factor(met, labels = c("No", "Yes"))) %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = criterion, fill = met)) +
  geom_bar(position = "stack",
           alpha=0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("No" = "red", "Yes" = "blue")) +
  labs(title = "",
       x = "", y = "") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank(),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 16))


```

---

# Trend .tiny[(Global Terrorism Data)]

```{r, fig.width=16}


gtdBase %>% 
  select(iyear, region_txt, crit1, crit2, crit3, targtype1_txt) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~na_if(., -99))) %>%
  count(iyear) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = iyear, y = n, alpha=0.6)) +
  geom_area(fill='red') +
  labs(title = "",
       x = "", y = "") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
              axis.text = element_text(size = 16)
)




```

---

# Trend by region .tiny[(Global Terrorism Data)]

```{r, fig.width=16}

library(ggrepel)

gtdBase %>% 
  select(iyear, country_txt, region_txt, 
         crit1, crit2, crit3, targtype1_txt) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~na_if(., -99)),
         region_recoded = case_when(
           country_txt %in% c("Afghanistan", "Pakistan") ~ "Middle East & Central Asia",
           region_txt %in% c("Central America & Caribbean", "South America") ~ "Latin America",
           region_txt == "North America" ~ "North America",
           region_txt %in% c("Western Europe", "Eastern Europe") ~ "Europe",
           region_txt %in% c("Middle East & North Africa", "Central Asia") ~ "Middle East & Central Asia",
           region_txt == "Sub-Saharan Africa" ~ "Africa",
           region_txt %in% c("South Asia", "Southeast Asia", "East Asia", "Australasia & Oceania") ~ "Asia & Pacific"
         )) %>%
  count(iyear, region_recoded) %>%
  group_by(region_recoded) %>%
  mutate(label = if_else(iyear == max(iyear), region_recoded, NA_character_)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = iyear, y = n, color = region_recoded)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_label_repel(aes(label = label), 
                   nudge_x = 1, na.rm = TRUE,
                   size = 8,
                   alpha=0.75) +
  labs(title = "", x = "", y = "") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
              axis.text = element_text(size = 16)
        )

```

---

# The Consequence .tiny[(Gadarian 2010)]

--

## - When exposed to threat, do people <br> change .red[foreign policy] attitudes?



---

background-image: url(https://raw.githubusercontent.com/textvulture/textvulture.github.io/refs/heads/master/images/Gadarian2010.png)
background-size: 50%
background-position: right

# The Consequence .tiny[(Gadarian 2010)]


## - When exposed to threat, do people <br> change .red[foreign policy] attitudes?


## - Yes, through .purple[emotion] ('scare')

--

## - Less of the news texts, <br> more of the .orange[images]
